repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Problem: No default mapping for :restart.

Solution: Map to ZR in defaults.

Closes: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/38942

comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1208,6 +1208,11 @@ ZZ			Write current file, if modified, and close the current
 							*ZQ*
 ZQ			Quit without checking for changes (same as ":q!").
 
+							*ZR*
+{count}ZR		Mapped to |:restart|. |default-mappings|
+			If a {count} is given, restarts without checking for 
+			changes (same as ":restart qall!").

comment_data.body:
```diff
+			If a {count} is given, restarts without checking for 
```
Trailing whitespace on this line (that's why the tests are failing).

comment_data.body:
count is optional so use [] 
```suggestion
[count]ZR		Mapped to |:restart|. |default-mappings|
			If a [count] is given, restarts without checking for
```

comment_data.body: this is an old github display bug, i think. will fix in docs pr if not
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1208,6 +1208,10 @@ ZZ			Write current file, if modified, and close the current
 							*ZQ*
 ZQ			Quit without checking for changes (same as ":q!").
 
+							*ZR*
+[count]ZR		Performs |:restart|. If a [count] is given, restarts
+			without checking for changes (":restart +qall!").

@@ -208,10 +207,74 @@ describe('TUI :restart', function()
   before_each(n.clear)
   after_each(n.check_close)
 
+  ---@param exp boolean Restart expected
+  ---@param sess table Session
+  ---@param addr string
+  local function assert_restarted(exp, sess, addr)
+    local s = sess
+    retry(nil, 5000, function()
+      if exp then
+        s:close()
+        s = n.connect(addr)
+      end
+
+      -- Cheesy but reliable: :restart drops "-- [files…]", so empty v:argf means restart happened.
+      -- TODO(justinmk): add `v:startreason`, `v:starttime`
+      local _, argf = s:request('nvim_eval', 'v:argf')
+      ok(vim.tbl_count(argf) == (exp and 0 or 1), exp and 'empty v:argf' or 'nonempty v:argf', argf)
+    end)
+  end
+
   it('validation', function()
     eq('Vim(restart):E481: No range allowed: :1restart', t.pcall_err(n.command, ':1restart'))
   end)
 
+  it('ZR', function()
+    -- Just exercise ZR, don't need to test all :restart functionality here.
+    t.skip(is_os('win'), 'FIXME: --listen not preserved by :restart on Windows #38539')

comment_regular_data.body: Also this is a nit but the commit message should have ZR in all caps, since mapping `zr` != mapping `ZR`.

> Also this is a nit but the commit message should have ZR in all caps, since mapping `zr` != mapping `ZR`.

Agreed, but unsure how strictly lintcommit should be followed.

> Agreed, but unsure how strictly lintcommit should be followed.

If my memory serves, it won't break the lint workflow. Even if it does, it can be merged without the check passing, since it's a logical exception.

> > Agreed, but unsure how strictly lintcommit should be followed.

> If my memory serves, it won't break the lint workflow. Even if it does, it can be merged without the check passing, since it's a logical exception.

All the other CI failures were painful to look at anyway.

title: feat(editor): ZR performs `:restart`
